revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Arida Gawa]][[ja:有田川]][[zh:有田川]]
{{Confusion|Aridagawa (Kushimoto Town)|x1=the [[Class B river]] flowing through '''[[Kushimoto Town]]''' in Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}} {{Other uses|the river flowing in the northern part of Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}} {{Infobox river |name = Aridagawa |image = [[File:Minoshima river in Arida.jpg|300px|Aridagawa, photographed March 1, 2007]] |image_caption = View upstream from Anteibashi in Minoshima, Arida City |river_system = [[Class B river system]] |length = 94 km |source1_elevation = 1008.5 m |discharge = -- |basin_size = 467.8 km² |source1 = [[Yangyūzan]] (Wakayama Prefecture) |mouth = {{ill|Kii Channel|en|Kii Channel|qid=Q1069095|lt=Kii Channel|1=Kii Channel}} (Wakayama Prefecture) |country = {{JPN|qq=}} Wakayama Prefecture }} '''Aridagawa''' (有田川) is a {{ill|River|en|River|qid=Q4022|lt=River|1=River}} in the western part of the [[Kii Peninsula]], flowing through the north-central area of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}. It is the main stream of the [[Class B river system]] in the region. == Geography == The river originates from Yangyūzan (楊柳山, elevation 1008.5 m) in [[Kōya Town]], [[Itō District]], Wakayama Prefecture. Flowing generally southwest, it turns west at Kanaya, where it merges with the Hayatsukidani River, before emptying into the {{ill|Kii Channel|en|Kii Channel|qid=Q1069095|lt=Kii Channel|1=Kii Channel}}<ref name="wakayama">{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/080400/keikaku/seibikeikaku_d/fil/arida_keikakku.pdf|title=二級河川 有田川水系河川整備計画 |publisher=Wakayama Prefecture |accessdate=2022-09-28|qq=}}</ref>. The basin area is approximately 467.8 km², and the river stretches about 94 km<ref name="wakayama" />. Land use in the Aridagawa basin consists of about 83% forests, 14% farmland, and 3% residential areas<ref name="wakayama" />. The basin's warm climate supports robust agriculture, with notable cultivation of citrus fruits such as oranges (e.g., [[Arida mikan]]), as well as grapes, tomatoes, and {{ill|sanshō|en|sanshō|qid=Q12929468|lt=sanshō|1=Sanshō}} pepper<ref name="wakayama" />. The upstream area, formerly Shimizu Town, is a major production area for sanshō, along with the upper Kishi River<ref name="wakayama" />. The river mouth, located in Arida City, hosts a broad waterway with generally excellent water quality throughout its length. Upstream from the [[Nikawa Dam]], the river is celebrated as a pristine stream, attracting campers and tourists from the nearby Hanshin region due to its gentle currents and numerous shallow areas. Additionally, Aridagawa is a popular spot for ayu fishing, with fishing season opening as early as May 1st in the prefecture. The upstream region is also home to [[Aragijima]], which has been selected as one of Japan's [[100 Terraced Rice Fields]]. == Municipalities Along the River == ; Wakayama Prefecture : [[Kōya Town]], [[Katsuragi Town]], [[Aridagawa Town]], [[Arida City]] == Parallel Transportation == === Railways === * {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|qid=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line|1=Kisei Main Line}} (Parallels the river between {{ill|Fujinami Station|en|Fujinami Station|qid=Q5507448|lt=Fujinami Station|1=Fujinami Station}} and {{ill|Minoshima Station|en|Minoshima Station|qid=Q3324376|lt=Minoshima Station|1=Minoshima Station}}, with crossings between Fujinami and {{ill|Kii-Miyahara Station|en|Kii-Miyahara Station|qid=Q3324370|lt=Kii-Miyahara Station|1=Kii-Miyahara Station}}). Previously, the [[Arida Railway]] line paralleled the river in Kibi Town before its closure on December 31, 2002. === Roads === [[File:Minoshima.jpg|thumb|The Aridagawa in Minoshima, Arida City, near its mouth. The road in the foreground is National Route 480.]] * [[:en:National Route 371|National Route 371]] – Parallels the uppermost portion of the river. * [[:en:National Route 480|National Route 480]] – Parallels the river from the upstream area to Arida Bridge near the mouth. * [[Wakayama Prefectural Route 22]] – Runs along the southern bank from the former Kibi Town Hall area to Fujinami Station on the Kisei Main Line. * [[:en:National Route 42|National Route 42]] – Runs along the southern bank from Fujinami Station to Arida Bridge. == Bridges == {{Div col|2|qq=}} * Tanado Bridge * Tanado Grand Bridge * Arida East Grand Bridge * Miyahara Bridge * Arida Central Grand Bridge * Yasuda Bridge * [[Anteibashi]] * Arida Bridge {{Div col end|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|qq=}}
== See Also == * [[Kishu Flood Disaster]] * [[Natural Dam]] {{Normdaten|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|有田川|version=98104138|comment=Imported full ja history}}
